title: Lautembergia coriacea
intro: Lautembergia coriacea, also known as Lautembergia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, pain, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lautembergia coriacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Lautembergia coriacea is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lautembergia coriacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This family is commonly known as the bellflower family. The Campanulaceae family consists of about 89 genera and over 2,300 species of flowering plants. Lautembergia coriacea is a small, delicate plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is classified within the family Campanulaceae due to its characteristic bell-shaped flowers and growth habits.
